headline_summary: Pakistan hosts Turkey/Egypt/Saudi talks on proposals to reopen Strait of Hormuz shipping (Reuters, Mar 29)
Two India-bound LPG tankers (BW Tyr, BW Elm) safely transit the Strait of Hormuz amid restrictions (Reuters, Mar 29)
Israel orders expansion of operations/security zone in southern Lebanon amid Hezbollah rocket fire (Reuters, Mar 29)
Chinese-operated container ships CSCL Indian Ocean and CSCL Arctic Ocean turn back from Hormuz after attempted exit; IRGC warnings reported (Reuters, Mar 27)
UAE's Emirates Global Aluminium reports significant damage at Al Taweelah site from Iranian missile/drone attacks (Reuters, Mar 28)
Egypt announces temporary austerity/remote-work measures to cope with economic fallout from Iran war and higher energy costs (Reuters, Mar 28)
Trump says US will pause attacks on Iran's energy plants for 10 days; indirect talks via Pakistan continue, per Reuters (Mar 26)
Markets: Reuters notes oil gains after Yemen's Houthis launch first attacks on Israel since war began, widening conflict (Mar 30)
risk_drivers: Strait of Hormuz transit restrictions and ad-hoc clearance regime
Regional spillover (Lebanon/Hezbollah front) alongside Iran-US/Israel conflict
Expanded strike footprint (UAE industrial facilities hit)
Diplomatic uncertainty around ceasefire proposals and maritime reopening mechanisms
Potential further widening (Houthis entering Israel strike set)

Energy: Elevated crude/LNG risk premia persist while Hormuz passage depends on case-by-case clearances; any convoy/consortium plan could reduce tail risk but is not operational yet.
Higher freight, insurance and rerouting costs likely endure; industrial supply chains (e.g., Gulf aluminium shipments) face disruption if exports remain constrained.
FX: Oil-importer EM FX (e.g., parts of Asia/MENA) faces renewed pressure if energy prices stay bid; safe-haven demand supports USD in risk-off episodes.
Gulf FX pegs may see reserve draw sensitivity if export receipts are constrained for longer.
Rates: Sticky energy-driven inflation risk can keep global rate-cut expectations constrained; wider credit spreads possible for high energy-import exposure sovereigns.
Any credible de-escalation/maritime reopening signal would likely tighten risk premia and support duration.
Equities: Risk-off rotations likely favor energy/defense while pressuring airlines, shipping, and energy-intensive industrials (e.g., metals) as supply/logistics costs rise.
Middle East regional equities face higher geopolitical discount; global cyclicals sensitive to shipping/energy disruptions.

sources.title: Pakistan hosts regional powers for Iran talks, with focus on Hormuz proposals
Two India-bound LPG tankers clear Strait of Hormuz, government says
Israel's Netanyahu orders expansion of southern Lebanon operations to halt Hezbollah rockets
Chinese ships halt attempt to exit Hormuz despite Iran safe passage assurances
Emirates Global Aluminium reports 'significant damage' from Iranian strikes
Egypt to slow down some state projects for two months amid Iran war, prime minister says
Trump pauses attacks on Iran's energy plants, says talks 'going well'
Gold rises as investors buy the dip, while fading rate-cut bets cap upside

events.title: Pakistan hosts Turkey, Egypt and Saudi Arabia talks on proposals to reopen Strait of Hormuz
events.implication: Regional diplomacy centers on mechanisms (fees/consortium) to restore Hormuz shipping; any progress could reduce energy tail risk.

events.title: Two India-bound LPG tankers (BW Tyr, BW Elm) transit the Strait of Hormuz under coordination
events.implication: Selective clearances indicate a partial, controlled reopening for 'non-hostile' vessels, but overall throughput remains constrained.

events.title: Chinese-operated container ships turn back from attempted Hormuz exit despite Iran assurances
events.implication: Turn-backs and reported IRGC warnings signal continued uncertainty and operational risk for commercial shipping even for 'friendly' flags.

events.title: Emirates Global Aluminium reports significant damage at Al Taweelah from Iranian missile/drone attacks
events.location_name: Al Taweelah, Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates
events.implication: Strike expansion into UAE raises escalation risk and disrupts metals supply; reinforces risk-off and higher insurance/logistics costs in Gulf.

events.title: Egypt announces temporary cuts to fuel allocations and slowdown of high-diesel state projects
events.implication: Energy-cost shock transmits to fiscal/real-economy measures in major MENA importer, raising sovereign and inflation sensitivity.

events.title: US says it will pause attacks on Iran's energy plants for 10 days as indirect talks continue
events.implication: Temporary pause is a de-escalation signal but fragile; negotiation outcomes directly affect Hormuz navigation and energy supply risk.

events.title: Markets report: Yemen's Houthis launch first attacks on Israel since war began, widening conflict
events.implication: Conflict widening raises risk of additional shipping/energy disruptions and supports a higher geopolitical premium in crude.

events.title: Israel orders expansion of operations in southern Lebanon to halt Hezbollah rockets
events.implication: Expanded operations increase multi-front escalation risk, potentially affecting regional risk premia and broader risk-off positioning.

links.from: US says it will pause attacks on Iran's energy plants for 10 days as indirect talks continue
links.to: Pakistan hosts Turkey, Egypt and Saudi Arabia talks on proposals to reopen Strait of Hormuz
links.why: A temporary pause in strikes may create space for regional intermediaries to advance ceasefire/shipping proposals.

links.from: Pakistan hosts Turkey, Egypt and Saudi Arabia talks on proposals to reopen Strait of Hormuz
links.to: Two India-bound LPG tankers (BW Tyr, BW Elm) transit the Strait of Hormuz under coordination
links.why: Diplomacy focused on reopening Hormuz aligns with reports of selective transits under coordination.

links.from: Chinese-operated container ships turn back from attempted Hormuz exit despite Iran assurances
links.why: Turn-backs imply shipping unreliability at a key energy chokepoint, sustaining oil risk premium.

links.from: Emirates Global Aluminium reports significant damage at Al Taweelah from Iranian missile/drone attacks
links.why: Strike expansion into the UAE increases perceived regional escalation and can drive risk-off positioning.

links.from: Markets report: Yemen's Houthis launch first attacks on Israel since war began, widening conflict
links.why: A wider conflict footprint tends to raise the probability of additional supply/logistics disruptions.
Higher oil prices worsen import bills and inflation for net oil importers, pressuring EM FX.
Energy inflation risk can keep policy restrictive and widen credit spreads for vulnerable sovereigns/corporates.
